prompt: Create a 12-second Manim scene that explains neurotransmitter reuptake after synaptic release. Show a presynaptic terminal releasing dots across the synaptic cleft, a few dots reaching receptors, and the remaining excess being pulled back by transporter arrows into the presynaptic side.
completion:
from manim import *

class NeurotransmitterReuptakeStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text('Reuptake Clears Excess Neurotransmitter', font_size=30).to_edge(UP)
        pre = RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.18, width=2.0, height=1.25, color=BLUE_D, fill_color=BLUE_D, fill_opacity=0.16).shift(LEFT * 3.0)
        post = RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.18, width=2.0, height=1.25, color=TEAL_D, fill_color=TEAL_D, fill_opacity=0.16).shift(RIGHT * 3.0)
        cleft = DashedLine(LEFT * 0.65 + DOWN * 1.2, LEFT * 0.65 + UP * 1.2, color=GRAY_B)
        pre_label = Text('presynaptic terminal', font_size=18, color=BLUE_D).next_to(pre, UP, buff=0.14)
        post_label = Text('postsynaptic side', font_size=18, color=TEAL_D).next_to(post, UP, buff=0.14)
        receptors = VGroup(*[RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.06, width=0.18, height=0.42, color=TEAL_D, fill_color=TEAL_D, fill_opacity=0.4).move_to(post.get_left() + RIGHT * 0.22 + UP * y) for y in [0.42, 0.0, -0.42]])
        transporters = VGroup(*[RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.06, width=0.18, height=0.42, color=ORANGE, fill_color=ORANGE, fill_opacity=0.4).move_to(pre.get_right() + LEFT * 0.22 + UP * y) for y in [0.42, 0.0, -0.42]])
        released = VGroup(*[Dot(pre.get_right() + RIGHT * (0.55 + 0.22 * i) + UP * y, color=YELLOW_D, radius=0.05) for i, y in enumerate([0.35, 0.05, -0.18, -0.42, 0.2])])
        receptor_dots = VGroup(*[Dot(receptors[i].get_center() + LEFT * 0.18, color=YELLOW_D, radius=0.05) for i in range(2)])
        reuptake_arrows = VGroup(*[CurvedArrow(released[i + 2].get_center(), transporters[i].get_center(), angle=0.45, color=ORANGE) for i in range(3)])
        caption = Text('Some transmitter binds receptors, but reuptake transporters pull the excess back out of the cleft.', font_size=21, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_group = VGroup(BackgroundRectangle(caption, fill_opacity=0.88, buff=0.14), caption)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), FadeIn(pre), FadeIn(post), Create(cleft), FadeIn(pre_label), FadeIn(post_label), run_time=2.0)
        self.play(FadeIn(receptors), FadeIn(transporters), run_time=1.2)
        self.play(LaggedStart(*[FadeIn(dot) for dot in released], lag_ratio=0.12), run_time=1.8)
        self.play(FadeIn(receptor_dots), LaggedStart(*[Create(arrow) for arrow in reuptake_arrows], lag_ratio=0.15), run_time=2.2)
        self.play(FadeIn(caption_group), Indicate(transporters, color=YELLOW_A), run_time=1.5)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 12-second Manim scene that explains lateral inhibition as contrast sharpening. Show five neighboring receptors with a bright center input on top, then an output row below where the center stays strong but the neighbors are suppressed, making the edge easier to detect.
completion:
from manim import *

class LateralInhibitionContrastStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text('Lateral Inhibition Sharpens Contrast', font_size=30).to_edge(UP)
        top_frame = Rectangle(width=6.4, height=1.8, color=GRAY_B).shift(UP * 0.85)
        bottom_frame = Rectangle(width=6.4, height=1.8, color=GRAY_B).shift(DOWN * 1.2)
        top_bars = VGroup(*[Rectangle(width=0.62, height=h, color=BLUE_D, fill_color=BLUE_D, fill_opacity=0.45) for h in [0.48, 0.82, 1.45, 0.82, 0.48]]).arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.3, aligned_edge=DOWN).move_to(top_frame.get_center() + DOWN * 0.35)
        bottom_bars = VGroup(*[Rectangle(width=0.62, height=h, color=ORANGE, fill_color=ORANGE, fill_opacity=0.45) for h in [0.18, 0.38, 1.28, 0.38, 0.18]]).arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.3, aligned_edge=DOWN).move_to(bottom_frame.get_center() + DOWN * 0.35)
        top_label = Text('receptor input', font_size=18, color=BLUE_D).next_to(top_frame, UP, buff=0.12)
        bottom_label = Text('after inhibition', font_size=18, color=ORANGE).next_to(bottom_frame, UP, buff=0.12)
        connectors = VGroup(*[Arrow(top_bars[i].get_bottom(), bottom_bars[i].get_top(), buff=0.12, color=GRAY_B) for i in range(5)])
        center_box = SurroundingRectangle(bottom_bars[2], color=YELLOW_D, buff=0.08)
        caption = Text('Neighboring signals inhibit one another, so the strongest location stands out more clearly than the raw input.', font_size=21, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_group = VGroup(BackgroundRectangle(caption, fill_opacity=0.88, buff=0.14), caption)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), FadeIn(top_frame), FadeIn(bottom_frame), FadeIn(top_label), FadeIn(bottom_label), run_time=1.8)
        self.play(LaggedStart(*[GrowFromEdge(bar, DOWN) for bar in top_bars], lag_ratio=0.12), run_time=1.8)
        self.play(LaggedStart(*[GrowArrow(arrow) for arrow in connectors], lag_ratio=0.08), LaggedStart(*[GrowFromEdge(bar, DOWN) for bar in bottom_bars], lag_ratio=0.12), run_time=2.1)
        self.play(Create(center_box), FadeIn(caption_group), Indicate(center_box, color=YELLOW_A), run_time=1.6)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 12-second Manim scene that explains population coding with three neuron tuning curves. Show broad response curves for three preferred stimuli, place a vertical line at one stimulus value, and add a compact bar readout so the combined activity pattern can be read at a glance.
completion:
from manim import *

class PopulationTuningCurveStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text('A Stimulus Is Represented By A Pattern Across Neurons', font_size=27).to_edge(UP)
        axes = Axes(x_range=[0, 100, 20], y_range=[0, 1.1, 0.5], x_length=6.6, y_length=4.2, tips=False).shift(LEFT * 1.5 + DOWN * 0.15)
        curve_a = axes.plot(lambda x: np.exp(-((x - 20) ** 2) / 450), x_range=[0, 100], color=BLUE_D)
        curve_b = axes.plot(lambda x: np.exp(-((x - 50) ** 2) / 450), x_range=[0, 100], color=TEAL_D)
        curve_c = axes.plot(lambda x: np.exp(-((x - 80) ** 2) / 450), x_range=[0, 100], color=ORANGE)
        stim_x = 58
        stim_line = DashedLine(axes.c2p(stim_x, 0), axes.c2p(stim_x, 1.02), color=YELLOW_D)
        dots = VGroup(Dot(axes.c2p(stim_x, np.exp(-((stim_x - 20) ** 2) / 450)), color=BLUE_D, radius=0.06), Dot(axes.c2p(stim_x, np.exp(-((stim_x - 50) ** 2) / 450)), color=TEAL_D, radius=0.06), Dot(axes.c2p(stim_x, np.exp(-((stim_x - 80) ** 2) / 450)), color=ORANGE, radius=0.06))
        bars = VGroup(Rectangle(width=0.42, height=0.45, color=BLUE_D, fill_color=BLUE_D, fill_opacity=0.45), Rectangle(width=0.42, height=1.28, color=TEAL_D, fill_color=TEAL_D, fill_opacity=0.45), Rectangle(width=0.42, height=0.72, color=ORANGE, fill_color=ORANGE, fill_opacity=0.45)).arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.3, aligned_edge=DOWN).to_edge(RIGHT).shift(DOWN * 0.55)
        bar_labels = VGroup(Text('A', font_size=18, color=BLUE_D).next_to(bars[0], DOWN, buff=0.1), Text('B', font_size=18, color=TEAL_D).next_to(bars[1], DOWN, buff=0.1), Text('C', font_size=18, color=ORANGE).next_to(bars[2], DOWN, buff=0.1))
        bar_title = Text('responses', font_size=18, color=WHITE).next_to(bars, UP, buff=0.14)
        caption = Text('No single neuron carries the whole answer. The stimulus is encoded by the response pattern across the population.', font_size=20, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_group = VGroup(BackgroundRectangle(caption, fill_opacity=0.88, buff=0.14), caption)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(axes), Create(curve_a), Create(curve_b), Create(curve_c), run_time=2.2)
        self.play(Create(stim_line), FadeIn(dots), run_time=1.5)
        self.play(LaggedStart(*[GrowFromEdge(bar, DOWN) for bar in bars], lag_ratio=0.12), FadeIn(bar_labels), FadeIn(bar_title), run_time=1.8)
        self.play(FadeIn(caption_group), Indicate(bars[1], color=YELLOW_A), run_time=1.5)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 12-second Manim scene that explains consumer surplus on a demand curve. Show a downward-sloping demand line, a market price line, the traded quantity, and shade the triangle between willingness to pay and the actual price.
completion:
from manim import *

class ConsumerSurplusStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text('Consumer Surplus Is Value Above The Market Price', font_size=29).to_edge(UP)
        axes = Axes(x_range=[0, 10, 1], y_range=[0, 10, 1], x_length=6.7, y_length=4.6, tips=False).shift(LEFT * 0.9 + DOWN * 0.1)
        demand = axes.plot(lambda x: 9 - 0.65 * x, x_range=[0.8, 9.0], color=BLUE_D)
        price_y = 4.6
        q_star = (9 - price_y) / 0.65
        price_line = Line(axes.c2p(0.8, price_y), axes.c2p(q_star, price_y), color=ORANGE, stroke_width=5)
        quantity_line = DashedLine(axes.c2p(q_star, 0), axes.c2p(q_star, price_y), color=GRAY_B)
        surplus = Polygon(axes.c2p(0.8, price_y), axes.c2p(q_star, price_y), axes.c2p(0.8, 9 - 0.65 * 0.8), color=GREEN_D, fill_opacity=0.3, stroke_width=0)
        labels = VGroup(Text('demand', font_size=20, color=BLUE_D).next_to(demand, UP, buff=0.12), Text('market price', font_size=20, color=ORANGE).next_to(price_line, DOWN, buff=0.15), Text('consumer surplus', font_size=20, color=GREEN_D).next_to(surplus, RIGHT, buff=0.15))
        caption = Text('Buyers up to the traded quantity would have paid more than the price they actually pay, and that gap becomes surplus.', font_size=20, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_group = VGroup(BackgroundRectangle(caption, fill_opacity=0.88, buff=0.14), caption)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(axes), Create(demand), run_time=2.0)
        self.play(Create(price_line), Create(quantity_line), FadeIn(labels[0]), FadeIn(labels[1]), run_time=1.8)
        self.play(FadeIn(surplus), FadeIn(labels[2]), run_time=1.5)
        self.play(FadeIn(caption_group), Indicate(surplus, color=YELLOW_A), run_time=1.5)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 12-second Manim scene that explains income inequality with a Lorenz curve. Show the line of perfect equality, a bowed Lorenz curve beneath it, and shade the gap between them so the inequality idea is visible without relying on formulas.
completion:
from manim import *

class LorenzCurveInequalityStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text('A Lorenz Curve Shows How Unevenly Income Is Shared', font_size=27).to_edge(UP)
        axes = Axes(x_range=[0, 100, 20], y_range=[0, 100, 20], x_length=6.8, y_length=4.8, tips=False).shift(LEFT * 0.9 + DOWN * 0.1)
        equality = Line(axes.c2p(0, 0), axes.c2p(100, 100), color=GRAY_B, stroke_width=4)
        lorenz = axes.plot(lambda x: 100 * (x / 100) ** 1.9, x_range=[0, 100], color=BLUE_D)
        area = Polygon(axes.c2p(0, 0), axes.c2p(20, 20), axes.c2p(40, 40), axes.c2p(60, 60), axes.c2p(80, 80), axes.c2p(100, 100), axes.c2p(100, 100), axes.c2p(80, 65.6), axes.c2p(60, 37.9), axes.c2p(40, 17.5), axes.c2p(20, 4.7), axes.c2p(0, 0), color=ORANGE, fill_opacity=0.22, stroke_width=0)
        labels = VGroup(Text('perfect equality', font_size=18, color=GRAY_B).next_to(equality, UP, buff=0.12), Text('Lorenz curve', font_size=18, color=BLUE_D).next_to(axes.c2p(68, 47), RIGHT, buff=0.12), Text('inequality gap', font_size=18, color=ORANGE).next_to(axes.c2p(58, 66), RIGHT, buff=0.12))
        caption = Text('The farther the Lorenz curve bends away from the equality line, the more concentrated the income becomes.', font_size=20, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_group = VGroup(BackgroundRectangle(caption, fill_opacity=0.88, buff=0.14), caption)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(axes), Create(equality), FadeIn(labels[0]), run_time=2.0)
        self.play(Create(lorenz), FadeIn(labels[1]), run_time=1.6)
        self.play(FadeIn(area), FadeIn(labels[2]), run_time=1.5)
        self.play(FadeIn(caption_group), Indicate(area, color=YELLOW_A), run_time=1.5)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 12-second Manim scene that explains diminishing marginal utility. Show a total-utility curve that rises but flattens as quantity increases, and add a side panel of marginal-gain bars that shrink with each additional unit.
completion:
from manim import *

class DiminishingMarginalUtilityStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text('Each Additional Unit Adds Less Utility Than The Previous One', font_size=26).to_edge(UP)
        axes = Axes(x_range=[0, 6, 1], y_range=[0, 11, 2], x_length=6.0, y_length=4.6, tips=False).shift(LEFT * 1.7 + DOWN * 0.1)
        utility = axes.plot(lambda x: 4.2 * np.log(x + 1), x_range=[0, 5.2], color=BLUE_D)
        dots = VGroup(*[Dot(axes.c2p(x, 4.2 * np.log(x + 1)), color=BLUE_D, radius=0.06) for x in [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]])
        side_frame = Rectangle(width=2.2, height=3.5, color=GRAY_B).to_edge(RIGHT).shift(DOWN * 0.1)
        bars = VGroup(Rectangle(width=0.36, height=1.55, color=ORANGE, fill_color=ORANGE, fill_opacity=0.45), Rectangle(width=0.36, height=1.12, color=ORANGE, fill_color=ORANGE, fill_opacity=0.45), Rectangle(width=0.36, height=0.76, color=ORANGE, fill_color=ORANGE, fill_opacity=0.45), Rectangle(width=0.36, height=0.48, color=ORANGE, fill_color=ORANGE, fill_opacity=0.45)).arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.18, aligned_edge=DOWN).move_to(side_frame.get_center() + DOWN * 0.35)
        side_title = Text('marginal gain', font_size=18, color=ORANGE).next_to(side_frame, UP, buff=0.12)
        caption = Text('Total utility keeps increasing, but the extra benefit from one more unit gets smaller each time.', font_size=20, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_group = VGroup(BackgroundRectangle(caption, fill_opacity=0.88, buff=0.14), caption)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(axes), Create(utility), FadeIn(dots), run_time=2.1)
        self.play(FadeIn(side_frame), FadeIn(side_title), LaggedStart(*[GrowFromEdge(bar, DOWN) for bar in bars], lag_ratio=0.14), run_time=1.9)
        self.play(FadeIn(caption_group), Indicate(bars, color=YELLOW_A), run_time=1.5)
        self.wait(1)
